Specimen information

Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired kidney function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and low specific gravity, with abnormal urine appearance and color, but no evidence of infection. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent hypoglycemia. Findings are suggestive of renal impairment with possible nephrotic-range proteinuria and metabolic derangements; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
